name: Luna 9
first: First soft-landing on the Moon; first photographs from the lunar surface
description: After 11 Soviet failures and 5 American Ranger missions designed only to crash, Luna 9 became the first spacecraft to soft-land on another world. Nine panoramic images returned over 8 minutes settled a long-running scientific debate: the Moon's surface was solid, not a sea of dust deep enough to swallow a lander. The mission lasted 3 days before battery depletion. Within months, every Moon-bound mission afterwards used soft-landing as the baseline.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Soft-landing at Oceanus Procellarum 1966-02-03 18:45 UTC. First soft-landing on another world.

events.label: FIRST IMAGES
events.note: Nine panoramic images returned in three transmission sessions.
